name: Río Segura Trail (GR 127)
name_original: Embalse de Azud de Ojós — circular en gravel desde Cieza por el río Segura
description: Cycle the difficult 17.7-mile Río Segura Trail (GR 127) in Valle de Ricote, featuring diverse terrain and river views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cieza are set within the fertile Segura River valley, characterized by extensive fruit orchards and surrounding mountains. The region features well-maintained Vías Verdes, offering accessible paths through diverse landscapes. Cyclists can explore routes that follow old railway lines and wind through agricultural areas. The terrain includes gentle gradients along riverbanks and more challenging sections with moderate elevation gains. Best touring cycling routes around Cieza The most popular touring cycling route is Río Segura Trail (GR 127), a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route follows the Segura River, offering views of its natural beauty and surrounding orchards. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Verde del Chicharra y Fortaleza de Ascoy — return from Cieza, a difficult 21.3 miles (34.2 km) path. This route utilizes an old railway line, providing a conditioned path through varied environments. Local touring cyclists also love the Archena Motorhome Park – Finca del Menju loop from Cieza, a 30.4 miles (48.9 km) trail leading through river areas and agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Cieza is defined by the Segura River valley, Vías Verdes, and extensive fruit orchards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Cieza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cieza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cieza?
A: Cieza offers a diverse selection of touring cycling routes, with over 30 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cieza?
A: The ideal time for touring cycling in Cieza is during late February to mid-March, coinciding with the spectacular 'Floración' (blossom season). During this period, the peach, plum, almond, and apricot trees burst into vibrant colors, creating a picturesque backdrop. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Cieza?
A: Yes, Cieza has several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. The region's Vías Verdes, such as sections of the Cieza Flowering Greenway, offer flat, well-maintained paths through orchards, providing a gentle and scenic cycling experience. While specific easy routes are available, many moderate routes can also be enjoyed at a leisurely pace.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Cieza?
A: Cieza's touring cycling routes wind through the fertile Segura River valley, characterized by extensive fruit orchards and surrounded by mountains. You'll encounter picturesque agricultural areas, especially during blossom season, and follow old railway lines transformed into greenways. Expect views of the river, cultivated fields, and distant peaks like the Sierra de Cabeza del Asno.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Cieza?
A: Yes, Cieza offers several circular touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14629477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archena Motorhome Park – Finca del Menju loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 48.9 km trail that takes you through river areas and agricultural landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38242614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarral River Beach – Acequia de la Andelma loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt;, covering 29.3 km.

Q: What are some longer touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Cieza?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for longer challenges can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14629477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archena Motorhome Park – Finca del Menju loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 49 km. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013500984/rio-segura-trail-gr-127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura Trail (GR 127)&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 28.5 km route offering significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I combine cycling with other outdoor activities in Cieza?
A: Absolutely! Cieza is a hub for outdoor activities. You can combine cycling with hiking on trails like 'La Isla – El Horno,' or enjoy water sports such as rafting, kayaking, and canoeing on the Segura River. The dramatic Almadenes Canyon also offers opportunities for adventure sports in its vicinity.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites I can visit along the touring routes in Cieza?
A: Cieza is rich in history. You can explore prehistoric sites like the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Levantine cave paintings at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Serreta Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or the archaeological remains of Medina Siyâsa, a significant Muslim settlement. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6289465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Almadenes Interpretation Center&lt;/a&gt; also provides insights into the region's natural and cultural heritage.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Cieza?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cieza are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the scenic beauty of the Segura River valley, the well-maintained Vías Verdes, and the unique experience of cycling through the fruit orchards, especially during the 'Floración'.

Q: Are there specific routes that showcase the famous 'Floración' in Cieza?
A: Yes, the Cieza Flowering Greenway is specifically designed to highlight the 'Floración' spectacle. This route, about 10 km long, winds through extensive fruit orchards, offering stunning views of the blossoming trees. It can also connect with the Vía Verde del Chicharra Cieza for a longer, immersive experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Cieza?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Cieza, especially near the starting points of popular Vías Verdes and other established routes. Specific parking information for individual routes can often be found on their respective komoot tour pages or local tourism websites.

Q: What are the typical durations and distances for touring cycling routes in Cieza?
A: Touring cycling routes in Cieza vary significantly in duration and distance. You can find shorter routes around 10 km, like sections of the Cieza Flowering Greenway, which might take less than an hour. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14629477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archena Motorhome Park – Finca del Menju loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt; (48.9 km), can take around 3 hours 40 minutes, offering options for all time commitments.

Q: Are there any Vías Verdes (Greenways) suitable for touring cyclists in Cieza?
A: Cieza is home to excellent Vías Verdes, which are ideal for touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017166123/via-verde-del-chicharra-y-fortaleza-de-ascoy-return-from-cieza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Chicharra y Fortaleza de Ascoy — return from Cieza&lt;/a&gt; follows an old railway line for 13.7 km, providing a conditioned path through varied environments. The Cieza Flowering Greenway is another notable greenway, perfect for experiencing the region's orchards.
tips: The old Finca del Meju was an authentic botanical garden that still has amazing corners.
Beautiful views of the Azud de Ojós Reservoir as we ride along Highway 520 in the Region of Murcia, near the town of Blanca.
It is a beautiful specimen that is part of the Parque de las Norias de Abarán. It can still be seen turning and raising water after so many years in service. It's worth a visit.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: The specific Komoot tour for the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) covers approximately 28.5 km and typically takes around 2 hours and 12 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. However, the full GR 127 is a long-distance route, spanning 168-173 km, which would require multiple days to complete.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse terrain. You'll encounter rocky and technical mountain trails, gravel, and poorly maintained surfaces in some sections, particularly in mountainous areas like the Almadenes Canyon. Other parts transition to flatter sections with tracks through farmlands, dedicated cycle lanes, and some asphalted stretches, especially closer to urban areas and within the Ricote Valley.

faqs.question: Is the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The overall difficulty of the GR 127 is generally considered easy to moderate due to following the gentle gradients of the river. However, this varies significantly by stage. The initial sections, such as those through the Almadenes Canyon, are considerably more challenging and demand good fitness and technical riding skills. Later stages, especially within the Ricote Valley, are flatter and more accessible for all skill levels. Beginners should research specific sections or consider shorter, easier segments.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in scenery and landmarks. You'll pass by the picturesque Azud de Ojós Reservoir, the historic Noria of the Hoya de Don García, and the scenic Finca del Menju. Other highlights include the impressive Almadenes Canyon, the unique rice fields of Calasparra, and the cultural site of the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be very hot in the Murcia region, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, and ensuring you carry plenty of water. Winters are typically mild, but some sections might be cooler or wetter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: Parking availability varies along the extensive GR 127. For the specific Komoot tour starting near Cieza, you can often find parking in or near the towns that the trail passes through. It's advisable to check local parking options in Cieza or Ojós, depending on your chosen starting point, as dedicated trail parking might be limited in some rural sections.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on most sections of the Río Segura Trail (GR 127), especially in rural areas. However, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, particularly when passing through farmlands, near livestock, or in more populated areas. Always clean up after your pet and be mindful of other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) or the Valle de Ricote?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) or to access the general areas of the Valle de Ricote. The trail is publicly accessible. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions or natural parks along the route, they might have their own entry requirements or fees.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially in the sections that pass through towns and villages like Cieza, Blanca, Ojós, and Archena, you will find cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and find water. However, some more remote sections, particularly in the initial stages or through canyons, may have limited services, so it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: Does the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) connect with other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) intersects with several other routes. Notably, it shares significant portions with the Camino de Santiago del Azahar and the Río Segura trail itself. It also connects with sections of the Transmurciana 22, Sendero de las Norias, and the Hoya de San Roque, offering opportunities to extend or vary your ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for cycling the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, a lightweight dual-suspension bicycle is recommended for comfort and capability. Essential items include a repair kit, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a first-aid kit. Due to potentially poor or deteriorated signage in some sections, carrying a reliable map or using a GPS device is highly advisable.

waypoints.name: Noria of the Hoya de Don García
